rationale.R1.1: The delegation is a positioning task; the contribution claim ('first design to deliver a credibly identified causal estimate…') and gap statement are the structural spine of the artifact. Validating that the thesis is correctly framed against each thread is a central expected responsibility.
rationale.R1.4: 'Position this against prior work' is definitionally a novelty-assessment task. Whether the delta over (i) quasi-experimental methods, (ii) descriptive crowdsourcing studies, and (iii) pre-LLM quality-control work is real, non-overlapping, and correctly characterised is load-bearing — an incorrect delta claim would directly invalidate the delegation.
rationale.R1.7: Credible positioning depends on accurate coverage and attribution of the three literature threads. Missing adjacent IV-in-platform-settings papers, mischaracterising the descriptive LLM-crowdsourcing literature, or omitting post-LLM annotator-quality work would undermine the 'no prior work occupies this point' claim and is therefore a central expected responsibility.
